encounter.reason: Treatment monitoring labs and hepatic ultrasound results review
encounter.location: Bridgetown Recovery Center, 920 SE Sandy Blvd, Portland, OR 97214

conditions.name: alcohol use disorder, moderate severity
conditions.note: patient making progress with reduced consumption and reduced cravings on naltrexone but has not yet achieved complete abstinence

conditions.name: bipolar II disorder
conditions.note: managed by outpatient psychiatry (Dr. Okafor), stable on lamotrigine and quetiapine

medications.name: naltrexone 50 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one 50 mg tablet by mouth once daily

medications.name: lamotrigine 200 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one 200 mg tablet by mouth once daily

medications.name: quetiapine 50 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one 50 mg tablet by mouth at bedtime

document_references.description: Lab visit results review documenting CMP with liver enzymes and GGT results showing improving liver enzyme trend (AST 38 from 52, ALT 44 from 61, GGT 62 from 89), negative urine drug screen, review of unremarkable hepatic ultrasound from 01/23/2023, and clinical update on patient's progress in IOP with reduced alcohol consumption on naltrexone though not yet fully abstinent. Plan to continue naltrexone and IOP, repeat liver enzymes at program completion.

diagnostic_reports.type: comprehensive metabolic panel with liver enzymes and GGT
diagnostic_reports.description: CMP with majority of components (sodium, potassium, chloride, CO2, BUN, creatinine, glucose, calcium, total protein, albumin, total bilirubin, alkaline phosphatase) reported as WNL without discrete numeric values. AST, ALT, and GGT have discrete values but three values alone are insufficient to constitute a credible CMP panel with results. Report documents overall improving hepatic function trend during alcohol use disorder treatment.

Hepatic ultrasound performed 01/23/2023, results reviewed at this encounter. Findings: liver parenchyma normal, no hepatic steatosis, no focal lesions identified, no biliary dilation. Impression: unremarkable study.
